examples.title: BMW Waste Classification Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to classify biomedical waste based on source and characteristics
examples.code:
PROCEDURE ClassifyBMW(waste_item, source)
 IF waste_item contains recognizable human tissue OR body parts THEN
 RETURN "Human Anatomical Waste"
 ELSE IF waste_item contains recognizable animal tissue OR carcasses THEN
 RETURN "Animal Anatomical Waste"
 ELSE IF waste_item can cause puncture/cut AND is used for injection THEN
 RETURN "Sharps"
 ELSE IF waste_item contains pathogens OR is contaminated with blood/fluids THEN
 RETURN "Infectious/Soiled Waste"
 ELSE IF waste_item is expired drug OR chemotherapy agent THEN
 RETURN "Pharmaceutical/Cytotoxic Waste"
 ELSE IF waste_item contains chemicals OR heavy metals THEN
 RETURN "Chemical Waste"
 ELSE IF waste_item contains radioactive isotopes THEN
 RETURN "Radioactive Waste"
 ELSE IF waste_item is glass material THEN
 RETURN "Discarded Glassware"
 ELSE
 RETURN "General Waste"
 END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This algorithm helps healthcare workers identify the correct BMW category based on visual inspection and source information. Proper classification is the first step in BMW management.

examples.title: BMW Segregation Decision Tree
examples.description: Step-by-step segregation process for healthcare workers at point of generation
examples.code:
PROCEDURE SegregateWaste(waste_description)
 STEP 1: Check if waste is body part or recognizable tissue
 IF YES → Place in YELLOW bag (Human Anatomical)
 ELSE → Proceed to STEP 2
 
 STEP 2: Check if waste is needle/syringe/sharp object
 IF YES → Place in WHITE puncture-proof container (Sharps)
 ELSE → Proceed to STEP 3
 
 STEP 3: Check if waste is contaminated with blood/body fluids
 IF YES → Place in RED bag (Infectious)
 ELSE → Proceed to STEP 4
 
 STEP 4: Check if waste is chemical or laboratory reagent
 IF YES → Place in BLUE bag/container (Chemical)
 ELSE → Proceed to STEP 5
 
 STEP 5: Check if waste is expired drug or vaccine
 IF YES → Place in YELLOW bag (Pharmaceutical)
 ELSE → Place in General Waste (Black/White bag)

 RETURN "Segregation Complete"
examples.explanation: This decision tree guides healthcare workers through proper segregation at source, which is critical to prevent cross-contamination and ensure appropriate treatment.

examples.title: BMW Generation Estimation for Hospital
examples.description: Algorithm to estimate BMW quantity based on bed capacity and type
examples.code:
FUNCTION EstimateBMW(bed_count, hospital_type)
 SET generation_rate = 0
 
 IF hospital_type == "multi-specialty" THEN
 SET generation_rate = 18 // kg/bed/month
 ELSE IF hospital_type == "nursing-home" THEN
 SET generation_rate = 12
 ELSE IF hospital_type == "primary-health-centre" THEN
 SET generation_rate = 5
 ELSE IF hospital_type == "diagnostic-lab" THEN
 SET generation_rate = 3 // per lab unit
 END IF
 
 SET total_BMW = bed_count * generation_rate
 
 // Estimate by category (approximate percentages)
 SET infectious_waste = total_BMW * 0.15
 SET pathological_waste = total_BMW * 0.05
 SET sharps = total_BMW * 0.02
 SET pharmaceutical = total_BMW * 0.03
 SET chemical = total_BMW * 0.02
 SET general_waste = total_BMW * 0.73
 
 RETURN {
 total: total_BMW,
 infectious: infectious_waste,
 pathological: pathological_waste,
 sharps: sharps,
 pharmaceutical: pharmaceutical,
 chemical: chemical,
 general: general_waste
 }
examples.explanation: This function helps hospital administrators estimate BMW generation for planning storage, transportation, and treatment facilities. Actual rates vary based on clinical activities.
